--- title: "Workers' health under climate change: Vector-borne diseases" description: Series of nine infographics describing the main risks to workers' health related to climate change and the environment [International Labour Organization (ILO) 2024](https://www.ilo.org/publications/ensuring-safety-and-health-work-changing-climate) date: 2026-02-06 date-modified: 2026-02-13 draft: false draft-mode: visible image: img/vectorborne_logo_02.png execute: echo: false categories: - Climate change - Workers Health - Tableau lang: en format: html: page-layout: full # code-tools: true embed-resources: true lightbox: true toc: false author: - name: Edgar Rodriguez-Huerta url: https://www.nottingham.ac.uk/research/beacons-of-excellence/rights-lab/our-team/edgar-rodríguez-huerta/index.aspx affiliation: University of Nottingham, Rights Lab affiliation-url: https://www.nottingham.ac.uk/research/beacons-of-excellence/rights-lab/index.aspx orcid: 0000-0002-6887-0040 --- ## Dengue in Brazil In 2024, Brazil exceeded its worst-case projection for dengue cases in June 2024, with a record 5.5 million infections reported, representing a 240 per cent increase from the 1.6 million cases recorded in 2023, which was already among the highest on record [^2]. ### Our vector-borne diseases Score **Vector-borne diseases** score is estimated by integrating Climate Hazard Index (Health) related to the occurrence of dengue, zika and chikungunya arboviruses (mean sceanrio for 2030)[^3], Incidence of dengue per 100,000 inhabitants[^4] Data visualization summarizes the analysis of excessive heat in **Brazil**, disaggregated by **municipality, population, and crops**, to identify spatial differences and exposure to vector-borne dieases (dengue) hotspots across dimensions. By translating complex climate and socio-environmental data into accessible visual formats, data visualization supports better decision-making, enabling targeted interventions, risk prioritization, and policies that advance decent work in agriculture. Vector-borne diseases is one of the main risks to workers' health related to climate change and the environment[^1] --- ![](img/04_viz_vector.png){width=100% height=100%} ### Download {.appendix} {{< downloadthis img/viz_vector.pdf dname=clidewo_climate_hazard_vectorborne label="Download the pdf" icon=file-earmark-pdf type=Warning >}} --- [^1]: International Labour Organization (ILO). *Ensuring safety and health at work in a changing climate*. [https://www.ilo.org/global/topics/safety-and-health-at-work/resources-library/publications/WCMS_893111/lang--en/index.htm](https://www.ilo.org/global/topics/safety-and-health-at-work/resources-library/publications/WCMS_893111/lang--en/index.htm) [^2]: Dengue outbreak in Brazil 2024 [https://www.dengue.com/Dengue-outbreak-in-Brazil-2024](https://www.dengue.com/Dengue-outbreak-in-Brazil-2024) [^3]: AdaptaBrasil [https://sistema.adaptabrasil.mcti.gov.br/](https://sistema.adaptabrasil.mcti.gov.br/) [^4]: Indice de Desenvolvimento Sustentável das Cidades Brasil [https://idsc.cidadessustentaveis.org.br/](https://idsc.cidadessustentaveis.org.br/) #### Share it on social media: ```{=html}
